q: What is the Ethereum network and who created it?

options.A: A Bitcoin fork created by Gavin Wood to speed up mining rewards.
options.B: A permissioned ledger built by David Rutter for regulated banks.
options.C: An open-source, programmable blockchain for smart contracts, created by Vitalik Buterin.
options.D: A closed stablecoin network created by Satoshi Nakamoto in 2013.

q: What problems did Vitalik Buterin see with the Bitcoin network for the future of the internet of value (Web3)?

options.A: It used proof-of-stake, which was too centralized and energy-hungry.
options.B: It was inefficient, lacked scalability, and had a limited scripting language.
options.C: It allowed too many cryptoassets, making its scripting language overly complex.
options.D: It relied on notaries and permissioned validation instead of open mining.

q: What is ether (ETH) and what role does it play on the Ethereum network?

options.A: A governance token used to vote on decisions related to the stablecoin DAI.
options.B: A non-fungible token representing ownership of unique dapps on the network.
options.C: A stablecoin pegged to the US dollar and backed by fiat reserves.
options.D: Ethereum's native token, the crypto fuel used to buy 'gas' to run dapps.

q: What is a smart contract? What is a decentralized application (dapp)? How are smart contracts and dapps related to LEGO?

options.A: Code that executes without human intervention; a dapp is built from these LEGO pieces.
options.B: A physical contract stored on paper; dapps are the LEGO blocks used to build it.
options.C: A front-end interface; dapps are the underlying code, and DAOs are the LEGO bricks.
options.D: A mining reward program; dapps are the LEGO instructions telling miners what to build.

q: What is a token and what are the different categories used by dapps?

options.A: A physical coin; categories are fiat, gold, cryptocurrency, and algorithmic.
options.B: A digital asset on a blockchain, broadly fungible (ERC-20) or non-fungible (ERC-721).
options.C: A mining reward, categorized as PoW tokens, PoS tokens, and gas tokens.
options.D: A governance right only, split into payment, security, and bridge tokens.

q: What is a decentralized autonomous organization (DAO) and what happened to the one created by Slock.It?

options.A: A physical corporation; Slock.It's DAO was shut down by Swiss regulators.
options.B: A stablecoin issuer; Slock.It's DAO lost its peg and collapsed to $0.10.
options.C: A mining pool; Slock.It's DAO was delisted after a 51% attack on Ethereum.
options.D: A virtual organization run by smart contracts; Slock.It's DAO was hacked and drained of ETH.

q: What is the purpose of a stablecoin and what are the different types? What distinguishes Tether from DAI?

options.A: A token pegged to another asset to reduce volatility; Tether is centralized, DAI decentralized.
options.B: A token pegged to gold only; Tether is decentralized, DAI centralized.
options.C: A non-fungible token for unique assets; both Tether and DAI are managed by MakerDAO.
options.D: A governance token granting voting rights; Tether and DAI are both algorithmic.

q: What is decentralized finance (DeFi) and what are the main activities across different blockchains?

options.A: A single lending product controlled by banks, offered mainly on the Bitcoin blockchain.
options.B: A permissioned ledger for regulated banks, running mostly on R3 Corda and Tron.
options.C: A decentralized financial system on blockchains, with DEXes, lending, and liquidity staking as top activities.
options.D: A centralized exchange network dominated by BSC, followed by Ethereum and Tron.

q: What is total value locked and how it is measured?

options.A: The total market cap of all cryptocurrencies, measured officially by the SEC.
options.B: The dollar value of crypto locked in smart contracts, pools, or staked, tracked by DefiLlama.
options.C: The number of ETH burned through slashing, reported quarterly by the Ethereum Foundation.
options.D: The gas fees paid across the network, measured by CoinMarketCap in real time.

q: Which DeFi activities do you find most interesting and why?

options.A: Only proof-of-work mining and block validation, which secure the Ethereum network.
options.B: Chiefly permissioned notary settlement and back-office clearing on R3 Corda.
options.C: Primarily NFT breeding games like CryptoKitties and digital art auctions.
options.D: DEXes, collateralized debt positions, liquidity staking, and derivatives as 'crypto LEGO'.
